meta.homeTitle: Hipolit Badowski | Platform & DevOps Engineer in Vienna
meta.homeDescription: Hipolit Badowski helps teams build dependable cloud platforms, reviewable delivery paths, and operable production systems.
meta.blogDescription: Field notes on platform engineering, delivery systems, Kubernetes, and production operations.

hero.eyebrow: Independent platform & DevOps engineer · Vienna / remote
hero.title: Safer change. Calmer operations.
hero.lede: I help teams turn fragile infrastructure and release handoffs into dependable platforms, reviewable delivery paths, and useful operational context.

proof.label: Platform
proof.value: Cloud and Kubernetes with clear ownership

proof.label: Operations
proof.value: Signals and runbooks that hold up under pressure

hero.portraitAlt: Hipolit Badowski working with a team at a table with laptops
hero.portraitBody: I work alongside people carrying production responsibility—close enough to improve the system, and careful enough to leave it understandable.

items.label: Release friction
items.value: Deployments have too many handoffs or too little certainty.

items.label: Platform drift
items.value: Infrastructure has outgrown the rules that once kept it understandable.

items.label: Operational gaps
items.value: On-call work exposes missing signals, ownership, or recovery context.

work.title: Three ways to make production work less fragile.
work.intro: The useful work begins where ambiguity turns into operational risk: the platform shape, the release path, and the information available when something fails.

items.label: Platform foundations
items.title: Give the platform a clear, usable shape.
items.body: Clarify environment boundaries, workload ownership, and the operating context that keeps cloud infrastructure understandable.

items.label: Dependable delivery
items.title: Turn changes into repeatable releases.
items.body: Replace fragile manual steps with reviewable workflows, traceable releases, and rollback points that do not depend on memory.

items.label: Operable systems
items.title: Make the next incident easier to understand.
items.body: Improve the signals, alerts, and operating notes that help teams diagnose failure without adding a theatre of dashboards.

build.title: Skill Mania: portable workflows for coding agents.
build.body: A public repository that keeps reusable agent skills in one canonical source, then packages them for Codex, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot.

method.title: Make the smallest change that removes real risk.
method.body: Infrastructure work is most useful when it leaves clearer boundaries, fewer surprise paths, and enough context for the next person to make a safe decision.

steps.title: Trace the system as it runs today.
steps.body: Find the release path, ownership edges, and failure points before proposing another layer.

steps.title: Make one safe, reviewable improvement.
steps.body: Simplify the path first; automate only the work that should be repeatable.

steps.title: Make the next decision less expensive.
steps.body: Record the operating notes, rollback path, and open questions where the team will use them.

notes.title: Writing from the operating edge.
notes.intro: Short, practical notes about the decisions that make platforms easier to change and production easier to understand.

contact.title: Start with the system that feels risky to touch.
contact.body: Send the current stack, the friction, and what better would look like. I’ll give you a direct view of where I can help and where I would be cautious.
contact.contextValue: Current stack, pain point, and a practical desired outcome
contact.terrainValue: Cloud platforms, CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and operations

blog.title: Field notes for people operating real systems.
blog.intro: Platform engineering, delivery, Kubernetes, and the practical work of keeping production understandable.

notFound.title: This path does not lead anywhere.
notFound.body: The address may be old, or the page may have moved during the rebuild.
